Basic Steps in Playing Sounds

If you understand the fundamentals outlined in Basic Concepts of Direct Music, you're ready to implement a rudimentary soundtrack in an application. After learning these few simple steps, you can go on to explore other parts of the library that will give you as much control over the soundtrack as you need.

To produce a sound, an application needs to do the following:

1. Create and initialize the performance

Most applications have a single performance object. You create it by calling DirectMusicCreateInstance and obtaining the IDirectMusicPerformance8 interface. Then call IDirectMusicPerformance8::InitAudioX. This method can set up a default audiopath.

2. Create the loader

Using DirectMusicCreateInstance, obtain an IDirectMusicLoader8 interface. You need to do this only once, and you should keep the same loader object for the life of the application.

3. Load a segment

Call IDirectMusicLoader8::SetSearchDirectory so that the loader can find the data files. Then call IDirectMusicLoader8::LoadObjectFromFile to load a segment and obtain its IDirectMusicSegment8 interface.

4. Play the segment

Pass the segment pointer to IDirectMusicPerformance8::PlaySegmentEx.

For a more detailed look at this process, see the following topics:

  • Loading Audio Data
  • Playing Sounds
